overall_assessment: The corrected RR-AUTH-04 baseline appears materially hardened and non-regressive within the bounded package, but closure should wait for explicit proof that the consume-before-mutate reset contract cannot combine with issuance cooldowns into a recovery lockout and that password reset still enforces the canonical password policy.
recommended_path: Resolve the documented reset-reissue timing and password-policy traceability gaps, or record explicit accepted debt for them, before treating the rerun as closure-grade.

findings.title: The fail-safe token burn contract is not paired with proof that immediate recovery remains possible after a post-consume mutation failure.
findings.rationale: The package explicitly states that reset tokens are consumed before password mutation succeeds and that any subsequent failure requires reissue, while also describing tenant-scoped cooldown and slot identities. The bounded evidence proves token burn on post-consume persistence failure, but it does not show whether the same user can immediately request a replacement token after that server-side failure or whether cooldown enforcement creates a temporary recovery lockout. That leaves a residual abuse-control versus operability timing risk in the exact failure mode this slice elevates as canonical behavior.
findings.suggested_action: Add corrected-baseline evidence that a post-consume password persistence failure either bypasses or safely resets the issuance cooldown for the affected principal, or document the intended lockout window and operator recovery contract as explicit accepted debt.

findings.title: The rerun package does not explicitly prove that reset-password mutation still enforces the canonical password policy.
findings.rationale: The dispatch scope for this security rerun calls out residual password-policy risk, but the package evidence is concentrated on fail-closed auth posture, token lifecycle hardening, and risk-matrix registration. None of the promoted invariants or proof surfaces explicitly state that the reset path is validated against the same canonical password requirements used elsewhere. Without that traceability, the package leaves a review-visible gap between the stated rerun focus and the bounded proof set.
findings.suggested_action: Attach a direct proof surface showing the reset-password path enforces the canonical password policy, or narrow the rerun scope statement so password-policy is no longer represented as covered by this package.
